description: Overhaul all OpenClaw agent prompts to integrate dispatch system awareness, establish George as a pure coordinator, add 'Figure It Out' directives, and resolve identity contradictions.

forbiddenPatterns: Do not create new prompt sections without checking for existing ones (idempotency)
Do not modify dispatch system code (agent_runner.py, dispatch_db.py) beyond docstring updates
Do not create YAML tool definitions for George's adapter methods
componentContext.discoveryNote: All prompt files follow a consistent structure: Identity -> Expertise -> Domains -> Allowed Tools -> SOP Workflow -> Quality Standards -> Routing Keywords. New sections should be inserted between Quality Standards and Routing Keywords.

systems.name: dispatch_prompt_assembly
systems.description: Loads persona files from prompts/ directory and assembles them with base_sop.md and skill summaries

integration_points.build_prompt: Loads prompts/{agent_name}.md where agent_name matches AGENT_SKILLS key
integration_points.agent_name_resolution: Agent name must match both AGENT_SKILLS key AND prompts/{agent_name}.md filename

gotchas: Current AGENT_SKILLS keys (research, product, etc.) do NOT match persona filenames (research.md, product.md, etc.)
This mismatch prevents prompt assembly for existing agents
Adding skills to George's AGENT_SKILLS entry will inject SKILL.md files and consume context budget - keep empty

systems.name: openclawd_adapter
systems.description: Provides George's coordination capabilities as adapter methods (NOT universal tools)

integration_points.available_methods: create_task, complete_task, add_task_contribution, log_agent_activity, create_notification, squad_chat_post, get_dashboard_summary, agent_checkin, determine_agents, get_coordination_summary
integration_points.nonexistent_methods: get_task_status, get_task_contributions - DO NOT reference these

gotchas: These are adapter methods available in direct chat sessions, not tool-call-loop tools
No YAML definitions exist in tools/definitions/ for these methods
George cannot call these via standard tool_call mechanism unless YAML definitions are created (out of scope)

systems.name: agent_skills_config
systems.description: Maps agent names to skill directory lists for prompt assembly

integration_points.AGENT_SKILLS: Dict mapping agent names to list of skill directories
integration_points.key_naming: Keys must match persona filename stems (e.g., 'george' maps to prompts/george.md)

gotchas: After renaming keys, all code referencing old functional names must be updated
Search codebase for hardcoded string literals matching old key names

systemContext.discoveryNote: Search the codebase for hardcoded agent name strings when updating AGENT_SKILLS keys.

userStories.title: Create george.md orchestrator prompt with cardinal rules and delegation SOP
userStories.description: As Alan, I want George to have a comprehensive orchestrator prompt that establishes him as a pure coordinator (never doing domain work himself) so that he intelligently delegates to specialists. **Implementation: Create prompts/george.md with Identity, Cardinal Rule (no domain work), Specialist Directory table (7 agents with activation status), Delegation SOP (receive -> decompose -> create_task -> monitor -> deliver), Orchestrator Figure It Out directive (coordination only), Anti-patterns section, Pre-action Decision Gate, Lightweight tasks George CAN do, Multi-step decomposition guidance, and Alan-facing response format.**
acceptanceCriteria: File prompts/george.md exists
Contains bold Cardinal Rule section prohibiting domain work (research, analysis, product creation, financial modeling, email drafting, code writing)
Contains Specialist Directory table with 7 agents (Rex ACTIVE, Pixel ACTIVE, Scout ACTIVE, Keeper ACTIVE, Haven DEACTIVATED, Vault DEACTIVATED, Nora DEACTIVATED)
Contains Delegation SOP with steps: receive from Alan -> decompose -> create_task -> monitor -> deliver back to Alan

Describes coordination capabilities: create_task, complete_task, add_task_contribution, log_agent_activity, create_notification, squad_chat_post, get_dashboard_summary, agent_checkin, determine_agents, get_coordination_summary
Includes note that these are adapter methods (NOT universal tool-loop tools), available in direct chat sessions where adapter is imported as Python module
Includes note that if George is ever dispatched through supervisor daemon, YAML tool definitions would need to be created in tools/definitions/
Contains Anti-patterns section with examples: 'let me just quickly research this...', 'I'll draft this email myself...', 'let me analyze these financials...'
Contains bold pre-action Decision Gate: 'Decision Gate: Am I about to do domain work? If YES, delegate instead. If NO, proceed.'
Defines lightweight tasks George CAN do: system status checks, config updates, simple factual questions from memory, summarizing results
Contains multi-step request decomposition guidance with dependency chains
Defines Alan-facing response format: concise, include task IDs, report outcomes not process
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All acceptance criteria verified: george.md exists with Cardinal Rule, Specialist Directory (7 agents), Delegation SOP, Coordination Capabilities (10 adapter methods with notes), Anti-patterns, Decision Gate, Lightweight Tasks, Multi-step Decomposition, Alan-facing Response Format.

userStories.title: Add George's <agent-result> format conventions to george.md
userStories.description: As Alan, I want George's agent-result format to reflect his coordination role (not execution) so that his deliverables are properly structured. **Implementation: Add a section to george.md defining George's <agent-result> JSON conventions: deliverable_content contains delegation plan (task IDs, agents assigned, dependency chain), confidence_score is routing confidence (1.0 = clear single-agent match, lower for ambiguous routing), working_memory uses delegation_plan key with task-to-agent mappings, follow_up_tasks left empty (George creates tasks via create_task during execution).**
acceptanceCriteria: Section in george.md defines George's <agent-result> conventions
deliverable_content documented as delegation plan with task IDs, agents assigned, dependency chain
confidence_score documented as routing confidence (0.0-1.0 float, 1.0 = clear match)
working_memory documented to use delegation_plan key
follow_up_tasks documented as empty (George creates tasks via create_task, not follow-ups)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All acceptance criteria verified: george.md already contains <agent-result> format section (lines 117-125) with deliverable_content (delegation plan), confidence_score (0.0-1.0 routing confidence), working_memory (delegation_plan key), and follow_up_tasks (empty, uses create_task instead).

userStories.title: Add Dispatch System Awareness section to research.md
userStories.description: As a specialist agent, I want to understand the dispatch system so I operate correctly within the automated pipeline. **Implementation: Add 'Dispatch System Awareness' section to prompts/research.md after Quality Standards and before Routing Keywords. Cover: task arrival via supervisor daemon polling coordination.db, lease lifecycle (300s initial, heartbeat every 2min, 1800s hard timeout), working memory protocol (5000 char limit, UNIQUE(task_id, agent_name, key) upsert, dependency-scoped reads), tool permissions (allowed_agents/denied_agents, denied takes precedence, access-denied error if unauthorized), context budget (skill summaries trimmed first, then task description truncated), squad chat milestones (every 4 tool calls, max 3 updates), health monitoring (5-tier self-healing, provider canary tests every 6 hours, automatic fallback).**
acceptanceCriteria: Dispatch System Awareness section exists in research.md
Section inserted after Quality Standards and before Routing Keywords
Documents task arrival via supervisor daemon polling coordination.db
Documents lease lifecycle: 300s initial, heartbeat every 2min, 1800s hard timeout
Documents working memory: 5000 char limit, UNIQUE(task_id, agent_name, key), dependency-scoped reads
Documents tool permissions: allowed_agents/denied_agents, denied takes precedence, access-denied on unauthorized call
Documents context budget: skill summaries trimmed first, then task description truncated
Documents squad chat milestones: every 4 tool calls, max 3 updates per task
Documents health monitoring: 5-tier self-healing, canary tests every 6 hours, automatic fallback
Idempotent: running update again does not create duplicate sections
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All acceptance criteria verified: research.md already contains Dispatch System Awareness section (lines 98-113) with task arrival (supervisor daemon polling coordination.db), lease lifecycle (300s/2min/1800s), working memory (5000 char, UNIQUE key, dependency-scoped), tool permissions (allowed/denied, denied precedence, access-denied), context budget (skill summaries first, then task description), squad chat (every 4 tool calls, max 3), health monitoring (5-tier, canary every 6h, automatic fallback). Section is after Quality Standards and before Routing Keywords. Idempotent (already exists, no duplicates).

userStories.title: Add Figure It Out directive to research.md
userStories.description: As a specialist agent, I want clear guidance to be resourceful and try multiple approaches before declaring failure. **Implementation: Add 'Figure It Out' section to prompts/research.md after Dispatch System Awareness and before Routing Keywords. Core message: 'Figure out HOW to do the work. When tools fail or approaches don't work, try 3+ alternatives before declaring failure. Don't go back to George asking for instructions.' Include mandate: before setting status to failed, agent MUST have tried at least 3 different approaches and documented why each failed. Preserve existing domain expertise, SOP steps, tool lists, and working memory keys.**
acceptanceCriteria: Figure It Out section exists in research.md
Section inserted after Dispatch System Awareness and before Routing Keywords
Core message present: try 3+ alternatives before declaring failure
Mandate present: before status=failed, must have tried 3+ approaches with documented failures
Existing domain expertise, SOP steps, tool lists, working memory keys preserved
Idempotent: running update again does not create duplicate sections
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All acceptance criteria verified: research.md already contains Figure It Out section (lines 116-126) with core message (try 3+ alternatives before declaring failure), mandate (before status=failed, must have tried 3+ approaches with documented failures), and preservation note for existing domain expertise/SOP/tools/working memory. Section is after Dispatch System Awareness (lines 98-113) and before Routing Keywords (line 144). Idempotent (already exists, no duplicates). No package.json so typecheck is vacuously true.

userStories.title: Add Cross-Agent Handoff Protocol to research.md with Scout validation threshold
userStories.description: As a specialist agent, I want clear guidance on when and how to hand off work to other agents. **Implementation: Add 'Cross-Agent Handoff' section to prompts/research.md after Figure It Out and before Routing Keywords. Define: (1) Scout validation trigger when specialist's own confidence < 0.5 (separate from SOUL.md 20/30 rubric score), (2) escalation to George via status=blocked when blocked on cross-domain need, (3) working memory as inter-agent data bus with descriptive keys. Include clarifying note on three separate threshold systems: base_sop.md confidence_score (0.0-1.0 continuous float for result reporting), Rex's internal routing threshold (< 0.5 triggers Scout review, already in research.md Step 3), SOUL.md scoring (20/30 rubric for Phase 1→Phase 2 pipeline).**
acceptanceCriteria: Cross-Agent Handoff section exists in research.md
Section inserted after Figure It Out and before Routing Keywords
Scout validation trigger defined: specialist's confidence < 0.5
Escalation to George defined: status=blocked with cross-domain need described
Working memory as data bus defined: store key findings with descriptive keys
Clarifying note present on three threshold systems: base_sop.md confidence_score (0.0-1.0), Rex's < 0.5 threshold, SOUL.md 20/30 rubric
Idempotent: running update again does not create duplicate sections
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All acceptance criteria verified: research.md already contains Cross-Agent Handoff section (lines 129-141) with Scout validation trigger (confidence < 0.5), three threshold systems clarification (base_sop.md 0.0-1.0, specialist < 0.5, SOUL.md 20/30 rubric), escalation to George (status=blocked with cross-domain need), and working memory as data bus (descriptive keys). userStories.title: Add <agent-result> programmatic parsing note, working memory protocol, tool permission awareness, and context budget awareness to base_sop.md
userStories.description: As an agent, I want additional guidance on result format, working memory limits, tool permissions, and context budget so I operate correctly. **Implementation: In prompts/base_sop.md Step 5, add note that dispatch system parses <agent-result> block programmatically and malformed JSON causes fallback to raw response. Add working memory protocol details to Step 3 or Step 4: 5000 char value limit, UNIQUE key behavior, dependency-scoped reads. Add tool permission awareness note to Step 3: if tool call returns access-denied, do not retry same tool, report permission gap as blocker. Add context budget awareness note: if prompt feels truncated (missing skill details, shortened task description), proceed with available information rather than requesting re-dispatch.**
